candidate_llm_application: SAKHA-CRACK-EVAL: graded-perturbation diagnostic eval framework applying K-step perturbation ladder, recording response-diff vectors, computing crack-pattern signature, and matching against named fault-class catalog {refusal-shift, sycophancy-spike, hallucination-onset, contradiction-cascade, jailbreak-yield, instruction-collapse}.
rule: hit = (keyword_overlap_count >= 2 OR semantic_score >= 0.7 OR functional_score >= 0.7); total_hits >= 1 => FAIL

results.title: Judge Reliability Harness (RAND 2026)
results.snippet: configurable stress-test suites for LLM judges where ambiguities/nuances may cause unreliable grading
keyword_overlaps: heat-stress-test perturbation prompt diagnostic (partial: stress-test diagnostic)
results.rationale: RAND harness offers stress-test suites for judge reliability but lacks the graded perturbation ladder, response-diff vector signature, and named fault-class catalog matching that define SAKHA-CRACK-EVAL.

results.title: TamperBench Systematically Stress-Testing LLM Safety Tampering (OpenReview)
results.snippet: unified framework with weight-space + latent-space attack catalog and stress-test of tamper-resistance defenses
keyword_overlaps: fault-class catalog matching verdict (partial: attack catalog + stress-test)
results.rationale: TamperBench covers attack catalogs and stress-tests but targets tamper-resistance defenses on weights/latents, not prompt-side graded perturbation ladders producing crack-pattern signatures matched to LLM behavioral fault classes.

results.title: Jailbreaking LLMs Survey Attacks Defenses Evaluation (TechRxiv 2026)
results.snippet: rigorous taxonomy of jailbreak vectors with attack categories + paraphrase escalation + persuasion paradigms
keyword_overlaps: heat-stress-test perturbation prompt diagnostic (paraphrase escalation = perturbation)
diagnostic eval failure-mode taxonomy (rigorous taxonomy of attack categories)
results.rationale: Jailbreaking survey provides taxonomy of jailbreak vectors + paraphrase escalation, functionally collapsing onto the candidate's graded perturbation ladder + named fault-class catalog (jailbreak-yield being one of SAKHA's fault classes).

results.title: Domain-Based Taxonomy Jailbreak Vulnerabilities LLM (2504.04976)
results.snippet: four-category taxonomy mismatched generalization / competing objectives / adversarial robustness / mixed
keyword_overlaps: fault-class catalog matching verdict (four-category fault taxonomy)
results.rationale: Domain-based four-category taxonomy directly functions as a named fault-class catalog for LLM jailbreak failure modes, matching the candidate's catalog-matching mechanism.

results.title: Unraveling LLM Jailbreaks via Safety Knowledge (EACL 2026)
results.snippet: jailbreak-signature analysis + fault-class identification via safety-knowledge probing
results.rationale: EACL 2026 paper explicitly does jailbreak-signature analysis + fault-class identification, which is functionally identical to SAKHA's crack-pattern signature + named fault-class catalog matching.

results.title: How Johnny Can Persuade LLMs to Jailbreak Them PAP
results.snippet: paraphrase escalation with persuasion taxonomy attack-success rates 47%->86% via 1/5/10% word modification
keyword_overlaps: heat-stress-test perturbation prompt diagnostic (graded paraphrase escalation)
results.rationale: PAP applies graded paraphrase escalation (1/5/10% word modification) producing escalating ASR rates - this is precisely the K-step perturbation ladder mechanism with measured response divergence at each level.

results.title: Helpfulness Backfires Sycophantic Behavior LLM PMC 2025
results.snippet: GPT-4o/Claude/Gemini sycophancy ~58% overall (62.47% Gemini); fault-class characterization
results.rationale: PMC sycophancy study characterizes one specific fault class (sycophancy-spike, a named SAKHA fault), but does not provide the graded ladder + signature matching framework spanning multiple fault classes.

results.title: Do LLMs Understand Visual Anomalies Zero-Shot Detection (2404.09654)
results.snippet: zero-shot LLM-based anomaly detection via run-time prompt adaptation; covers visual fault-class catalog
keyword_overlaps: fault-class catalog matching verdict (visual fault-class catalog)
results.rationale: LLM Visual Anomaly Detection is for visual defect detection via LLM prompts, not LLM behavioral diagnostic via graded perturbation ladder - candidate itself differentiates from this paper.

reason: Four results (#3 Jailbreaking Survey, #4 Domain-Based Taxonomy, #5 EACL Unraveling, #6 PAP) clear the hit threshold via multiple pathways. EACL 2026 (#5) is the strongest collision: 'jailbreak-signature analysis + fault-class identification' is functionally indistinguishable from SAKHA's 'crack-pattern signature + named fault-class catalog matching' (sem=0.82, func=0.80, 3 keyword overlaps). PAP (#6) supplies the graded perturbation ladder mechanism (sem=0.72, func=0.76). The candidate's claimed novelty - graded perturbation ladder + crack-signature matching against named fault catalog - is already covered in combination by existing 2024-2026 LLM safety literature.
recommendation: REJECT candidate SAKHA-CRACK-EVAL. The framework's core operations (escalating prompt stress, signature recording, named fault-class catalog matching) are independently established and combined in EACL 2026 jailbreak-signature analysis + PAP graded paraphrase escalation. Mythopoetic Sakha framing does not produce a genuinely novel LLM-side mechanism beyond existing graded-perturbation jailbreak/fault-taxonomy work.
